questions.question: What is the core philosophy behind the Lean Email-First stack?

options.text: Use as many tools as possible for maximum coverage
Master email as the highest-ROI channel before adding complexity — the best stack is the one you'll actually use for 90 days
Always start with LinkedIn before email
Spend at least $500/month for professional results

questions.explanation: The Stack Paradox states that the best outreach stack isn't the one with the most features — it's the one you'll actually use consistently for 90 days. Email is the highest-ROI channel for solo founders. Master it before adding complexity.

questions.question: In the Lean Email-First stack, why is Instantly chosen over Smartlead as the core platform?

options.text: $2/month cheaper, simpler UI, better built-in A/B testing, and Lead Finder included (saves $39-49/month on Apollo)

questions.explanation: Instantly wins for the lean stack because it's $2/month cheaper, has a simpler UI (faster to learn), better built-in A/B testing, and includes Lead Finder (1,000 B2B leads/month), which saves $39-49/month that would otherwise go to Apollo. Total stack cost: ~$120/month.

questions.question: How many domains and what daily send volume does the reference stack recommend for getting started?

options.text: 3 domains with 3 inboxes each, starting at 25 emails/day per domain after 14 days of warmup

questions.explanation: The reference implementation uses 3 domains (main + 2 secondary), starts warmup on Day 1, waits 14 days, then launches at 25 emails/day per domain. By Week 4, this reaches approximately 375 emails per week with safe deliverability.

questions.question: Most solo founders waste their first $200/month by buying overlapping tools before sending a single email.
questions.explanation: True. The lesson describes the common mistake: buying Lemlist ($99), Apollo ($49), Loom ($15), then adding Instantly ($37) for warmup — reaching $200 with overlapping tools and zero emails sent. The lean approach: start with one core platform, prove the channel works, then scale when revenue justifies it.

questions.question: Design your lean email-first stack with a maximum budget of $150/month. List each tool, its monthly cost, and what function it serves. Explain your 30-day ramp plan including warmup timeline and target send volume by week 4.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response stays within $150/month budget, includes core components (email platform, warmup, enrichment), and describes a realistic 30-day ramp plan with specific warmup timeline (14+ days) and gradual volume increase. Look for understanding that warmup must complete before cold sends begin.
